123	namespace Scheme
124	
125	namespace RationalMap
126	
127	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
128	
129	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
130	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
131	open subset of `X`).
132	
133	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
134	
135	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
136	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
137	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
138	
139	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
140	
141	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
142	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
143	
144	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
145	§I.3, p. 16). -/
146	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
147	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
148	
149	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
150	of an open). -/
151	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
152	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
153	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
154	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
155	
156	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
157	
158	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
159	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
160	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
161	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
162	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
163	`f.domain`.
164	
165	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
166	conclusion). -/
167	
168	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
169	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
170	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
171	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
172	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
173	
174	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
175	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
176	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
177	
178	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
179	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
180	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
181	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
182	
183	end RationalMap
184	
185	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
186	
187	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
188	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
189	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
190	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
191	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
192	
193	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
194	
195	/-! ### Stacks 00TT scaffolding (Lane M↓ iter-191 first scaffold)
196	
197	The Mathlib gap "smooth over `\bar k` ⟹ stalk is a regular local ring"
198	(Stacks tag `00TT`) is structured here as a four-stage pipeline:
199	
200	* **Stage 1** — smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk (Mathlib instance
201	  `AlgebraicGeometry.instFlatOfSmooth` + `Flat.stalkMap`).
202	* **Stage 2** — smooth at a point ⟹ exists a standard smooth presentation
203	  on an affine neighbourhood (Stacks tag `00T7`, packaged in Mathlib at
204	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`).
205	* **Stage 3** — the polynomial generators of the standard smooth
206	  presentation form a regular sequence after localising at any prime
207	  (Stacks `02JK`/`07BV`; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
208	* **Stage 4** — a Noetherian local ring whose maximal ideal is generated
209	  by a regular sequence of length equal to its Krull dimension is a
210	  regular local ring (`IsRegularLocalRing.of_regularSequence`; Stacks
211	  `00OE`/Matsumura 19.2; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
212	
213	Stages 1-2 are landed axiom-clean as named helpers below. Stages 3-4
214	remain a single scoped `sorry` inside the main theorem
215	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` until either the cotangent /
216	sheaf-of-relative-differentials route (Mathlib upstream) lands or the
217	project-side `02JK`/`00OE` chain is built (iter-192+). -/
218	
219	/-- **Stage 1 (Stacks 00TT, smooth ⟹ flat at stalk).** For a smooth
220	morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)` with `k̄` algebraically closed,
221	the induced ring map on stalks at every point `z : X.left` is flat. This
222	is the smooth ⟹ flat instance applied through the stalk-map flatness
223	characterisation `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`.
224	
225	Axiom-clean: composes the smooth ⟹ flat instance with the stalk-map
226	flatness lemma. No Mathlib gap. -/
227	private theorem stalkMap_flat_of_smooth
228	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
229	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
230	    [Smooth X.hom]
231	    (z : X.left) :
232	    ((X.hom.stalkMap z).hom).Flat :=
233	  AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.stalkMap X.hom z
234	
235	/-- **Stage 2 (Stacks 00T7, smooth ⟹ standard smooth presentation at a
236	point).** For a smooth morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)`, every
237	point `z : X.left` admits affine open neighbourhoods `U ⊆ Spec (.of k̄)`,
238	`V ⊆ X.left` with `z ∈ V` and `V ⊆ X.hom ⁻¹' U` such that the induced
239	ring map `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom` is `IsStandardSmooth`.
240	
241	Axiom-clean: re-exports `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`
242	(the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00T7`) at the project's
243	`Over (Spec (.of k̄))` calling convention. -/
244	private theorem exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth
245	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
246	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
247	    [Smooth X.hom]
248	    (z : X.left) :
249	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
250	      (V : X.left.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
251	      (_hzV : z ∈ V) (e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U),
252	      (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
253	  AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth X.hom z
254	
255	/-- **Stage 3 (Stacks 00TT, scheme-to-algebra bridge for the standard smooth
256	presentation).** Iter-192 axiom-clean intermediate helper: combines Stage 2's
257	existence-of-standard-smooth-presentation output with the `RingHom`-to-`Algebra`
258	bridge `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra`, plus the affine-open stalk
259	localisation `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`. Returns the full algebra-side
260	package needed by Stages 4-5 of the regularity proof: an affine neighbourhood
261	`V ∋ z`, an `Algebra Γ(Spec _, U) Γ(X.left, V)` instance under which
262	`Γ(X.left, V)` is `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth` over `Γ(Spec _, U)`, plus the
263	`IsLocalization.AtPrime` witness identifying the stalk at `z` with the
264	localisation of `Γ(X.left, V)` at the prime ideal of `z`.
265	
266	Axiom-clean: composition of `exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth` +
267	`RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` + `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`.
268	
269	This is the iter-192 Lane M↓ HARD BAR new axiom-clean helper: it packages the
270	"smooth ⟹ stalk is localisation of a standard-smooth Γ(Spec, U)-algebra"
271	content as a standalone declaration that downstream Stages 4-5 (the genuine
272	Stacks 02JK + 00OE Mathlib-gap chain) can consume directly. -/
273	private theorem exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth
274	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
275	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
276	    [Smooth X.hom]
277	    (z : X.left) :
278	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_hU : IsAffineOpen U)
279	      (V : X.left.Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V)
280	      (hzV : z ∈ V) (_e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U)
281	      (alg : Algebra Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) Γ(X.left, V)),
282	      @Algebra.IsStandardSmooth _ _ _ _ alg ∧
283	      letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
284	      IsLocalization.AtPrime
285	        (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := by
286	  obtain ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, hStdSmooth⟩ :=
287	    exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth X z
288	  refine ⟨U, hU, V, hV, hzV, e, (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.toAlgebra,
289	    hStdSmooth.toAlgebra, ?_⟩
290	  -- Localisation witness: the affine-open stalk is `Aₚ` for `p = primeIdealOf z`.
291	  letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
292	  exact hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩
293	
294	/-- **Stage 4 (Stacks 00T7(2), Kähler-differentials freeness).** Iter-192
295	axiom-clean intermediate helper: given an algebra-level
296	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S` instance (typically arising from
297	`Stage 3 = exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth`),
298	the module of Kähler differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` is `Module.Free` over `S`. The
299	basis is the `{d sᵢ}ᵢ` family witnessed by
300	`Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`.
301	
302	Axiom-clean: re-export of `Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential`
303	+ `Module.Free.of_basis`. -/
304	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
305	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
306	    [Algebra.IsStandardSmooth R S] :
307	    Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R]) := by
308	  obtain ⟨_, _, b, _⟩ :=
309	    (Algebra.IsStandardSmooth.iff_exists_basis_kaehlerDifferential
310	      (R := R) (S := S)).mp ‹_›
311	  exact Module.Free.of_basis b
312	
313	/-- **Stage 5a (Stacks 02JK, freeness transports through localisation, algebra-side).**
314	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` whose Kähler
315	differentials `Ω[S⁄R]` are `S`-free (e.g.\ when `S` is `R`-standard smooth via
316	`module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth`), the localisation `Sₘ` at any
317	submonoid `M ⊆ S` has Kähler differentials `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` which are `Sₘ`-free.
318	
319	Axiom-clean: composes `KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map` (Mathlib re-export)
320	with `Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule`. This is the substantive localisation-of-freeness
321	step that Stage 5 of the smooth ⟹ regular pipeline uses; extracted as a named helper
322	here so the main proof body of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` reads as a clean
323	chain of named lemmas rather than an inline `IsLocalizedModule` + `Module.free_of_*`
324	maneuver. -/
325	private theorem module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization
326	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
327	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
328	    [Module.Free S (Ω[S⁄R])]
329	    (M : Submonoid S)
330	    (Sₘ : Type u) [CommRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ] [Algebra S Sₘ]
331	    [IsScalarTower R S Sₘ] [IsLocalization M Sₘ] :
332	    Module.Free Sₘ (Ω[Sₘ⁄R]) := by
333	  haveI : IsLocalizedModule M (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ) :=
334	    KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule_map R S Sₘ M
335	  exact Module.free_of_isLocalizedModule (R := S) (Rₛ := Sₘ) (S := M)
336	    (M := Ω[S⁄R]) (Mₛ := Ω[Sₘ⁄R])
337	    (KaehlerDifferential.map R R S Sₘ)
338	
339	/-- **Stage 5b (Stacks 02JK + 00T7, rank computation through localisation, algebra-side).**
340	Iter-193 Lane M↓ axiom-clean substrate helper: for an `R`-algebra `S` that is
341	`IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n` over `R` and a localisation `Sₘ` at any
342	submonoid `M` (with `Sₘ` non-trivial), the `Sₘ`-rank of `Ω[Sₘ⁄R]` equals `n`. This
343	upgrades Stage 5a's freeness conclusion with a *specific* rank.
344	
345	Axiom-clean: composes `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.rank_kaehlerDifferential`
346	(Mathlib, gives `rank S Ω[S⁄R] = n`) with `Module.lift_rank_of_isLocalizedModule_of_free`
347	(Mathlib, transports module rank through `IsLocalizedModule`). The combination
348	provides the rank of the stalk-side Kähler differentials, which is one of the two
349	ingredients of the regularity conclusion at the stalk (the other ingredient is the
350	Stacks 00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula, still a Mathlib gap). -/
351	private theorem rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension
352	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
353	    {S : Type u} [CommRing S] [Nontrivial S] [Algebra R S]
354	    (n : ℕ) [hS : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S]
355	    (M : Submonoid S)

477	    cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
478	    {R Sₘ : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing Sₘ] [IsLocalRing Sₘ] [Algebra R Sₘ]
479	    [Algebra.FormallySmooth R Sₘ]

481	    [Subsingleton (Ω[IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])] :
482	    (RingHom.ker (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ))).Cotangent ≃ₗ[Sₘ]

484	  -- Surjectivity of `algebraMap Sₘ κ` is the residue surjection.
485	  have hSurj : Function.Surjective
486	      (algebraMap Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
487	    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
488	    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective
489	  -- Step 1: retraction → injection via `iff_split_injection`.
490	  have hInj : Function.Injective
491	      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
492	    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
493	      (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection
494	        (R := R) (P := Sₘ) (A := IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj).mp ‹_›
495	    refine Function.LeftInverse.injective (g := l) (fun x => ?_)
496	    have := LinearMap.congr_fun hl x
497	    simpa using this
498	  -- Step 2: `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` + exactness → surjection.
499	  have hExact :=
500	    KaehlerDifferential.exact_kerCotangentToTensor_mapBaseChange R Sₘ
501	      (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj
502	  have hSurjMap :
503	      Function.Surjective
504	        (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ
505	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
506	    intro x
507	    have h0 :
508	        KaehlerDifferential.mapBaseChange R Sₘ
509	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) x = 0 :=
510	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
511	    exact (hExact x).mp h0
512	  -- Step 3: bijective → linear equivalence.
513	  exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurjMap⟩

631	/-! ### Stage 6 sub-gap (ii.B) Stacks-00OE substrate (iter-200)
632	
633	The residual Mathlib gap for Stage 6 is the smooth-algebra Krull-dim formula:
634	for a standard-smooth `k`-algebra `S` of relative dim `n` and a maximal ideal
635	`m ⊂ S` corresponding to a closed point over an algebraically closed base,
636	`ringKrullDim (Localization.AtPrime S m) = n`.

638	The iter-200 `mathlib-analogist coe-stacks00oe` recipe ranks this as a 3-step
639	chain composing existing Mathlib pieces:
640	
641	* **Step 1** (`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`, trivial):
642	  rewrite `ringKrullDim (Localization.AtPrime S m) = m.height`.
643	* **Step 2** (polynomial-ring height): for the standard-smooth presentation
644	  `MvPolynomial ι k ↠ S` with kernel a regular sequence, the contracted
645	  maximal ideal `m' ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k` has height `#ι`. This is built
646	  axiom-clean below via induction on `Fin n` along Mathlib's
647	  `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one` + `MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv`
648	  + `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing`.
649	* **Step 3** (regular-sequence drop, Stacks 00SW / 00OW): the relations of the
650	  standard-smooth presentation form a regular sequence in the localization,
651	  consumed via `ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`. The
652	  Jacobian-criterion regular-sequence witness is the substantive residual
653	  obligation; landing it requires `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation.jacobian_isUnit`
654	  + `IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal`
655	  scaffolding which is iter-201+ Lane COE substrate.
656	
657	Iter-200 lands Step 2 fully axiom-clean (`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card`
658	+ general-finite-ι transport `MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard`),
659	the Step 1 named bridge (`ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`), and
660	the Step 3 quotient-form re-export
661	(`ringKrullDim_quotient_isRegular_eq_sub_length`) so the iter-201+ closure
662	collapses to building the Jacobian-regular-sequence witness alone. -/
663	
664	/-- **Step 1 bridge (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200).** Ergonomic re-export of
665	`IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height`: for a prime ideal `m ⊂ R` and
666	a localization `A = R_m`, `ringKrullDim A = m.height`. This is the boundary
667	translator inside `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` between
668	`Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight` (the iter-183 CoheightBridge bridge,
669	phrased in `ringKrullDim`) and the Mathlib polynomial-ring height arithmetic

673	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime
674	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (m : Ideal R) [m.IsPrime]
675	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra R A] [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
676	    ringKrullDim A = m.height :=
677	  IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A
678	
679	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), `Fin n` form.** For a
680	field `k` and a maximal ideal `m ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`, the height of
681	`m` equals `n`.
682	
683	This is the substantive Step 2 ingredient: combined with the standard-smooth
684	presentation surjection `MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k ↠ S`, it computes the
685	height of any contracted maximal ideal `m' = preimage(m) ⊂ MvPolynomial (Fin (#ι)) k`
686	explicitly. The closed-point case is the substantive one — every maximal ideal
687	of `MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` has height exactly `n` (independent of whether `k`
688	is algebraically closed; the Jacobson property suffices).
689	
690	Axiom-clean proof: induction on `n`. The base case `n = 0` is trivial (no
691	maximal-ideal content beyond `bot_le`). The inductive step transports `m` along
692	`MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv` to a maximal ideal `m'` of
693	`Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)`, contracts to `p = m' ∩ MvPolynomial (Fin n) k`
694	(maximal by `Polynomial.isMaximal_comap_C_of_isJacobsonRing` since
695	`MvPolynomial (Fin n) k` is Jacobson), and applies `Polynomial.height_eq_height_add_one`
696	followed by the inductive hypothesis on `p`. The upper bound follows from
697	`Ideal.height_le_ringKrullDim_of_ne_top` + `MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim_of_isNoetherianRing`. -/
698	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_ge_card_of_field
699	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (n : ℕ) :
700	    ∀ (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) [m.IsMaximal], (n : ℕ∞) ≤ m.height := by
701	  induction n with
702	  | zero => intro m _; exact_mod_cast bot_le
703	  | succ n ih =>
704	    intro m _
705	    let e := MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv k n
706	    let m' : Ideal (Polynomial (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k)) := Ideal.map e.toRingEquiv m
707	    haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e.toRingEquiv)
708	    let p : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin n) k) := Ideal.comap Polynomial.C m'

736	/-- **Step 2 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), general `[Finite ι]` form.**
737	For a field `k`, a finite index type `ι`, and a maximal ideal
738	`m ⊂ MvPolynomial ι k`, the height of `m` equals `Nat.card ι`.
739	
740	Axiom-clean: transports the `Fin n` form
741	`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card` along the canonical
742	`MvPolynomial.renameEquiv` induced by `(Finite.equivFin ι)`. -/
743	private theorem MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard.{v}
744	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (ιx : Type v) [Finite ιx]
745	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal] :
746	    m.height = Nat.card ιx := by
747	  classical
748	  haveI : Fintype ιx := Fintype.ofFinite ιx
749	  let σ : ιx ≃ Fin (Fintype.card ιx) := Fintype.equivFin ιx
750	  let e : MvPolynomial ιx k ≃+* MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k :=
751	    (MvPolynomial.renameEquiv k σ).toRingEquiv
752	  let m' : Ideal (MvPolynomial (Fin (Fintype.card ιx)) k) := Ideal.map e m
753	  haveI : m'.IsMaximal := Ideal.map_isMaximal_of_equiv (e := e)
754	  have hm_eq : m.height = m'.height := (RingEquiv.height_map e m).symm
755	  have hcard : Nat.card ιx = Fintype.card ιx := by simp [Nat.card_eq_fintype_card]
756	  rw [hm_eq, hcard]
757	  exact MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_card k (Fintype.card ιx) m'

760	localisation `A` of `MvPolynomial ιx k` at a maximal ideal `m`, the Krull
761	dimension of `A` equals `Nat.card ιx`.

763	This is the most direct project-side consumer route for Step 1 + Step 2: it
764	takes the polynomial-ring height equality (`m.height = Nat.card ιx` via
765	`MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard`) and pre-composes with the
766	Step 1 bridge (`ringKrullDim A = m.height` via

773	`Sₘ = A ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs`. -/
774	private theorem ringKrullDim_localization_atMaximal_MvPolynomial.{v}
775	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {ιx : Type v} [Finite ιx]
776	    (m : Ideal (MvPolynomial ιx k)) [m.IsMaximal]
777	    (A : Type*) [CommRing A] [Algebra (MvPolynomial ιx k) A]
778	    [IsLocalization.AtPrime A m] :
779	    ringKrullDim A = (Nat.card ιx : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
780	  rw [IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim_eq_height m A,
781	    MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard k ιx m]
782	  rfl

784	/-- **Step 3 substrate (Stacks 00OE chain, iter-200), parameterised additive form.**

785	For a Noetherian local ring `R'` with `ringKrullDim R' = a` (a natural number) and
786	a regular sequence `rs : List R'` of length `b`, the Krull dimension of the
787	quotient `R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs` plus `b` equals `a`.
788	
789	This is the parameterised version of Mathlib's
790	`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular` packaged in `WithBot ℕ∞`
791	arithmetic, with the natural-number values of dimension and length explicit. It is
792	the additive form preferred over a subtraction form because `WithBot ℕ∞` does not
793	carry a subtraction structure.
794	
795	The consumer of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` chains:
796	
797	* `R'` = the polynomial-ring localisation `R'_{m'} = (MvPolynomial ι k)_{m'}`,
798	* `hDim` (a = #ι) = `MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal_height_eq_natCard` composed with
799	  the Step 1 bridge `ringKrullDim_localization_eq_height_atPrime`,
800	* `hReg` = the Jacobian-criterion regular-sequence witness (residual Mathlib gap;
801	  iter-201+ Lane COE),
802	* `hLen` (b = #σ) = the cardinality of the regular sequence.

804	Then `ringKrullDim Sₘ = a − b = n` follows by cancellation from this lemma.

806	Axiom-clean: 3-line re-export. -/
807	private theorem ringKrullDim_quotient_add_eq_of_regular_sequence
808	    {R' : Type*} [CommRing R'] [IsNoetherianRing R'] [IsLocalRing R']
809	    (rs : List R') (hReg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R' rs)
810	    {a b : ℕ} (hDim : ringKrullDim R' = (a : WithBot ℕ∞))
811	    (hLen : rs.length = b) :
812	    ringKrullDim (R' ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (b : WithBot ℕ∞) = (a : WithBot ℕ∞) := by
813	  have h := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs hReg
814	  rw [hDim, hLen] at h
815	  exact h

843	/-- **Iter-201 Step A2 substrate, Mathlib gap-free part 1.** For a submersive

854	private theorem submersivePresentation_relation_cotangent_mk_linearIndependent
855	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
856	    {ix sx : Type*} [Finite sx]

860	        (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)) := by
861	  have h := P.basisCotangent.linearIndependent

863	        (⟨P.relation i, P.relation_mem_ker i⟩ : P.toExtension.ker)) = P.basisCotangent := by
864	    funext i
865	    exact (P.basisCotangent_apply i).symm
866	  rw [heq]

890	    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]

893	    (p : Ideal S) [p.IsPrime] :

1267	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/

1270	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1271	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1272	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1273	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1274	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
1275	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
1276	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:

1278	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
1279	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
1280	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
1281	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
1282	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
1283	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
1284	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
1285	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
1286	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz

1292	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by

1301	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
1302	
1303	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
1304	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
1305	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
1306	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
1307	
1308	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
1309	
1310	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1311	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1312	
1313	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1314	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1315	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1316	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1317	
1318	The proof combines two steps:
1319	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1320	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1321	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1322	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1323	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1324	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1325	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1326	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1327	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1328	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1329	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1330	
1331	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1332	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1333	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1334	
1335	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1336	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1337	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1338	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1339	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1340	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1341	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1342	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1343	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1344	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1345	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1346	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1347	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
1348	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1349	  -- Derived structural instance: the target Y.left is a separated scheme
1350	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine
1351	  -- hence separated over the terminal).
1352	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1353	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1354	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
1355	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
1356	  -- The substantive Milne 3.1 proof body reduces to showing Dom(f) = ⊤:
1357	  --
1358	  -- * Step 1 (codim-1 components of Z(f) ruled out) uses the valuative
1359	  --   criterion of properness on the complete target Y plus the DVR-stalk
1360	  --   structure at codim-1 points (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` above, gated
1361	  --   on `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6 Mathlib gap).
1362	  -- * Step 2 (codim-≥2 extension) uses the depth-≥2 local-cohomology
1363	  --   vanishing `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point (Stacks 0AVF), a
1364	  --   genuine Mathlib gap at commit `b80f227`. The codim-1-free hypothesis
1365	  --   `_hf` alone suffices for Step 1's conclusion but Step 2 requires the
1366	  --   local-cohomology / `Module.depth` bridge from
1367	  --   `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`).
1368	  --
1369	  -- Once `Dom(f) = ⊤` is established, the unique morphism extension is the
1370	  -- composition `X.left.topIso.inv ≫ (X.left.isoOfEq h).inv ≫ f.toPartialMap.hom`;
1371	  -- uniqueness follows from the reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1372	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` / `PartialMap.equiv_iff_of_isSeparated`).
1373	  --
1374	  -- Iter-200+ tracked: this lane is gated on Stacks 0AVF
1375	  -- (depth-≥2 H¹ vanishing) + `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6.

1378	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1379	
1380	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1381	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1382	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1383	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
1384	
1385	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1386	
1387	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1388	codim 1.**
1389	
1390	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1391	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1392	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1393	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1394	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1395	
1396	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1397	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1398	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1399	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1400	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1401	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1402

43	\section{Setup and motivation}
44	\label{sec:codim1_setup}
45	
46	Fix an algebraically closed field \(\bar k\) throughout. By a \emph{variety} over \(\bar k\)
47	we mean a geometrically integral, separated scheme of finite type over \(\bar k\)
48	(Milne's convention, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, p.~v); a variety is \emph{nonsingular}
49	(or \emph{smooth}) if its local ring at every closed point is regular. A variety is
50	\emph{complete} if it is proper over \(\bar k\).
51	
52	A \emph{rational map} \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) between varieties is the equivalence
53	class of a regular morphism \(\varphi_U \colon U \to Y\) defined on a dense open subset
54	\(U \subseteq X\), where two pairs \((U, \varphi_U)\), \((U', \varphi_{U'})\) are equivalent
55	if \(\varphi_U\) and \(\varphi_{U'}\) agree on \(U \cap U'\) (Milne~\S I.3, p.~16). The map \(f\)
56	is \emph{defined at} a point \(x \in X\) if there is some representative \((U, \varphi_U)\)
57	with \(x \in U\); the set of such \(x\) is an open subset \(\mathrm{Dom}(f) \subseteq X\),
58	the \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\).
59	
60	The motivating question of this chapter, following Milne~\S I.3, is:
61	\begin{quote}
62	  \emph{When does a rational map \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) extend to a regular
63	  morphism on all of \(X\)?}
64	\end{quote}
65	Without hypotheses on \(X\) or \(Y\) the question has no positive answer (Milne lists three
66	counterexamples on p.~16: the inclusion of a proper open into the ambient variety; the
67	rational map to the cuspidal cubic whose inverse fails to extend; the inverse of a
68	blow-up at a smooth point). The two structural inputs that buy unconditional extension
69	in our setting are:
70	\begin{itemize}
71	  \item \emph{Target completeness:} if \(Y\) is complete (proper over \(\bar k\)), the
72	    valuative criterion of properness forces the rational map to extend across every
73	    codim-\(1\) point of a normal source \(X\) (Milne Theorem~3.1).
74	  \item \emph{Target group structure:} if \(Y\) is a group variety, the difference map
75	    \(\Phi(x, y) := f(x) \cdot f(y)^{-1}\) on \(X \times X\) has a vanishing/pole divisor
76	    along the diagonal that controls the codimension of the indeterminacy locus
77	    (Milne Lemma~3.3).
78	\end{itemize}
79	For \(Y\) an abelian variety both conditions hold, and combining them yields Milne's
80	Theorem~3.2 (the headline of \cref{chap:Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension}). The
81	present chapter packages the two inputs separately:
82	\cref{thm:codim_one_extension} is Milne~3.1, and
83	\cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} is Milne~3.3. The Weil-divisor reformulation
84	\cref{thm:weil_divisor_obstruction} translates the codim-\(1\) extension obstruction
85	in Milne~3.1's proof into the order-at-a-prime-divisor language pinned in
86	\cref{chap:RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor}, which is the form the Lean prover consumes when
87	working with the project's \(\mathrm{Scheme.WeilDivisor}\) API.
88	
89	\section{The indeterminacy locus of a rational map}
90	\label{sec:indeterm_locus}
91	
92	\begin{definition}
93	
94	\leanok
95	  [Indeterminacy locus of a rational map]
96	  \label{def:indeterminacy_locus}
97	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus}
98	  % SOURCE: [Milne, Abelian Varieties], \S I.3, p.~16, the paragraph "Let \(V\) and \(W\)
99	  % be varieties over \(k\)\ldots" defining the domain of definition of a rational map
100	  % (read from references/abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 22).
101	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "A rational map \(\varphi\) is said to be \emph{defined} at a point
102	  % \(v\) of \(V\) if \(v \in U\) for some \((U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi\). The set \(U_1\) of
103	  % \(v\) at which \(\varphi\) is defined is open, and there is a regular map
104	  % \(\varphi_1 \colon U_1 \to W\) such that \((U_1, \varphi_1) \in \varphi\) --- clearly,
105	  % \(U_1 = \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in \varphi} U\) and we can define \(\varphi_1\) to be
106	  % the regular map such that \(\varphi_1 | U = \varphi_U\) for all $(U, \varphi_U) \in
107	  % \varphi$."
108	  \textit{Source: Milne, \emph{Abelian Varieties}, \S I.3, p.~16.}
109	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
110	  The \emph{domain of definition} of \(f\) is the open set
111	  \[
112	    \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;:=\; \bigcup_{(U, \varphi_U) \in f} U \;\subseteq\; X,
113	  \]
114	  i.e.\ the union of the dense opens of all representatives of \(f\). The
115	  \emph{indeterminacy locus} of \(f\) is the closed complement
116	  \[
117	    Z(f) \;:=\; X \smallsetminus \mathrm{Dom}(f) \;\subseteq\; X.
118	  \]
119	  The map \(f\) is regular (i.e.\ defined on all of \(X\)) if and only if \(Z(f) = \emptyset\).
120

149	  Let \(f \colon X \dashrightarrow Y\) be a rational map between varieties over \(\bar k\).
150	  We say \(f\) is \emph{codim-1-indeterminacy-free} (or has \emph{codimension-\(\geq 2\)

162	  empty or pure codim 1 from \cref{lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy} \(\Rightarrow\) \(Z(f)\)

170	  \(\eta \in X\) with \(\texttt{Order.coheight\ }\eta = 1\), $\eta \in
171	  \texttt{f.domain}\(. The encoding matches the standing \)\mathrm{Order.coheight}$
172	  convention pinned in \cref{def:prime_divisor}, so prime divisors of \(X\) and the
173	  hypothesis ``no codim-\(1\) indeterminacy'' use the same Mathlib idiom.

176	\section{Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point}
177	\label{sec:smooth_dvr}
178	
179	The codim-\(1\) extension argument in Milne's proof of Theorem~3.1 hinges on the local
180	ring at the generic point of a prime divisor being a discrete valuation ring (so that
181	the valuative criterion of properness applies). On a normal variety this is the
182	defining property of regularity in codimension one; on a smooth variety it is automatic.
183	
184	The smooth-implies-regular-local-stalk implication itself is the Jacobian-criterion
185	direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}; we package it as a separate sub-lemma so that
186	\cref{lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr} below reads cleanly as ``regular local of dimension
187	\(1\) is a DVR'', with the regularity prerequisite supplied by the sub-lemma.
188	
189	\subsection{Stages 5a/5b: K\"ahler-differentials localisation substrate (iter-193)}
190	\label{subsec:kaehler_localisation_substrate}
191	
192	The Jacobian-criterion direction of Stacks tag \texttt{00TT}
193	(\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring} below) factors on the Lean side
194	through a six-stage chain. Stages~1--2 (smooth \(\Rightarrow\) flat at a
195	stalk; smooth \(\Rightarrow\) admits a standard-smooth presentation
196	locally) are axiom-clean since iter-191; Stages~3--4 (the
197	scheme-to-algebra bridge for standard-smooth presentations; freeness of
198	\(\Omega_{S/R}\) for a standard-smooth algebra \(R \to S\)) are
199	axiom-clean since iter-192. The remaining Stages~5--6 reduce the
200	Stacks~00TT conclusion to a freeness plus rank computation on K\"ahler
201	differentials, transported through localisation at the prime defining
202	the stalk. Iter-193 packaged Stages~5a (transport of freeness) and 5b
203	(matching rank under localisation) as the two standalone helpers below;
204	Stage~6 (the remaining Stacks~00OE smooth-algebra dimension formula
205	plus Stacks~02JK cotangent--K\"ahler-over-a-field bridge) is the open
206	Mathlib gap, decomposed iter-198 into the three named sub-lemmas
207	\cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (6.A),
208	\cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (6.B), and
209	\cref{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly} (6.C) in
210	\cref{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition} immediately following.
211	
212	\begin{lemma}
213	
214	\leanok
215	  [Stage 5a --- K\"ahler-differentials freeness transports through localisation]
216	  \label{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
217	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.module_free_kaehlerDifferential_localization}
218	  Let \(R\) and \(S\) be commutative rings equipped with an \(R\)-algebra
219	  structure on \(S\), and assume that the module of K\"ahler differentials
220	  \(\Omega_{S/R}\) is free as an \(S\)-module. Let \(M \subseteq S\) be a
221	  submonoid and let \(S_M\) be a localisation of \(S\) at \(M\), so that
222	  \(R \to S \to S_M\) is a tower of \(R\)-algebras. Then the module of
223	  K\"ahler differentials \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) is free as an
224	  \(S_M\)-module.
225	\end{lemma}
226	
227	\begin{proof}
228	  The canonical comparison map
229	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) induced by the localisation map
230	  \(S \to S_M\) realises \(\Omega_{S_M/R}\) as the localisation of the
231	  \(S\)-module \(\Omega_{S/R}\) at \(M\); i.e.\ it satisfies Mathlib's
232	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} predicate along the underlying ring
233	  localisation. This is Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, packaged in Mathlib as
234	  \texttt{KaehlerDifferential.isLocalizedModule\_map}. Freeness of a
235	  module is preserved under localisation of modules via Mathlib's
236	  \texttt{Module.free\_of\_isLocalizedModule}; combining the two yields
237	  the conclusion.
238	\end{proof}
239	
240	\begin{lemma}
241	
242	\leanok
243	  [Stage 5b --- K\"ahler-differentials rank under localisation equals
244	  the relative dimension]
245	  \label{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
246	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.rank_kaehlerDifferential_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
247	  \uses{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}
248	  With notation as in \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization},
249	  assume in addition that the \(R\)-algebra \(S\) is \emph{standard
250	  smooth of relative dimension \(n\)} (Mathlib's
251	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension}\,\(n\)\,\(R\)\,\(S\),
252	  the algebra-side packaging of Stacks tag \texttt{00T7}(2)) and that
253	  the localisation \(S_M\) is non-trivial as a ring. Then the rank of
254	  \(\Omega_{S_M / R}\) over \(S_M\) equals \(n\).
255	\end{lemma}
256	
257	\begin{proof}
258	  Mathlib's
259	  \texttt{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.\linebreak rank\_kaehlerDifferential}
260	  asserts \(\mathrm{rank}_S(\Omega_{S/R}) = n\) under the
261	  standard-smooth hypothesis. The localisation map
262	  \(\Omega_{S/R} \to \Omega_{S_M/R}\) is an
263	  \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} along \(S \to S_M\) (Stacks tag
264	  \texttt{02JK}; cf.\ the proof of
265	  \cref{lem:module_free_kaehler_localization}), and Mathlib's
266	  \texttt{Module.lift\_rank\_of\_isLocalizedModule\_of\_free} transports
267	  module rank through an \texttt{IsLocalizedModule} up to
268	  \(\mathrm{Cardinal.lift}\). Since \(n\) is a natural number both
269	  ranks equal \(n\), so
270	  \(\mathrm{rank}_{S_M}(\Omega_{S_M / R}) = n\) as required.
271	\end{proof}

273	\subsection{Stage 6 sub-decomposition: 00OE + 02JK chain (iter-198)}
274	\label{subsec:stage6_subgap_decomposition}
275	
276	Stage 6 is the residual gap inside
277	\cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}'s six-stage chain: given Stages
278	1--5b (a standard-smooth presentation of an affine chart
279	\(R \to S\) at a point \(z\), with \(\Omega_{S/R}\) free of rank \(n\)
280	and \(\Omega_{S_\mathfrak q / R}\) free of rank \(n\) on the stalk
281	\(S_\mathfrak q = \mathcal O_{X, z}\)), one must conclude that
282	\(S_\mathfrak q\) is a regular local ring. The Stacks proof of tag
283	\texttt{00TT} ultimately reduces to two ingredients which Mathlib does
284	not yet ship at commit \texttt{b80f227}: (6.A) the smooth-algebra
285	Krull-dimension formula (Stacks tag \texttt{00OE}, in the algebra
286	chapter as Lemma~\texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}),
287	and (6.B) the cotangent / K\"ahler-differential bridge over a field
288	base (Stacks tag \texttt{02JK}, in the algebra chapter as
289	Lemma~\texttt{lemma-differential-seq} plus the separable-residue-field
290	refinement of Lemma~\texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}). The two
291	ingredients combine (6.C) into the regularity conclusion via
292	\(\dim S_\mathfrak q = \mathrm{rank}_{S_\mathfrak q}\Omega_{S_\mathfrak
293	q / R} = \dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\),
294	which is the cotangent-space characterisation of regularity that
295	Mathlib's \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing.iff\_finrank\_cotangentSpace}
296	already exposes. The next three lemmas package the three sub-gaps as
297	named iter-199+ prover targets.
298	
299	\begin{lemma}
300	  [Stage 6.A --- Smooth-algebra Krull-dimension formula (Stacks 00OE)]
301	  \label{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
302	  \lean{Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.ringKrullDim_localization_eq_relativeDimension}
303	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim}
304	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field
305	  % (canonically known as tag 00OE; tag-to-label cross-check not in the
306	  % local `tags/tags` file, see iter-198 writer report note) (read from
307	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L28206--28217 in section
308	  % "Dimension theory of finitely generated $k$-algebras").
309	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $k$ be a field. Let $S$ be a finite type $k$
310	  % algebra. Let $X = \Spec(S)$. Let $\mathfrak p \subset S$ be a prime
311	  % ideal, and let $x \in X$ be the corresponding point. Then we have
312	  % $\dim_x(X) = \dim(S_{\mathfrak p}) + \text{trdeg}_k\
313	  % \kappa(\mathfrak p)$."
314	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~00OE (\(=\) \texttt{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field}).}
315	  Let \(k\) be a field, let \(S\) be a finite-type \(k\)-algebra, and let
316	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\) be a prime ideal corresponding to a point
317	  \(x \in \mathrm{Spec}(S)\). Then
318	  \[
319	    \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)
320	    \;=\; \dim S_{\mathfrak q} + \mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q).
321	  \]
322	  In particular, if \(\mathfrak q\) is a maximal ideal (so \(x\) is a
323	  closed point) and \(k\) is algebraically closed, then
324	  \(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = k\) (Nullstellensatz), so
325	  \(\mathrm{trdeg}_k\,\kappa(\mathfrak q) = 0\) and
326	  \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q} = \dim_x(\mathrm{Spec}\,S)\).
327	
328	  \paragraph{Geometric application.} Applied to the iter-193 Stage~3
329	  setup --- \(k = \bar k\), \(S = \Gamma(X.\mathrm{left}, V)\) a
330	  standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra of relative dimension \(n\) (Stage
331	  3), and \(\mathfrak q\) the prime defining the point \(z \in V\) --- the
332	  formula yields \(\dim(\mathcal O_{X, z}) = \dim S_{\mathfrak q} = n -
333	  \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(z)\). For \(z\) a closed point this is
334	  simply \(n\); for the generic point of a codim-\(1\) subvariety
335	  (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) it is \(1\).
336	\end{lemma}
337	
338	\begin{lemma}
339	  [Stage 6.B --- Cotangent \(\leftrightarrow\) K\"ahler over a field (Stacks 02JK)]
340	  \label{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
341	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.cotangent_iso_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue}
342	  % \lean{...} updated iter-199 review: iter-199 prover landed the
343	  % closed-point iso as a project-local axiom-clean helper (private
344	  % theorem in AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme namespace). Companion siblings:
345	  %   cotangent_iso_maximalIdeal_residue_tensor_kaehler_of_formallySmooth_residue
346	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue
347	  %   finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue (closed-point bundled)
348	  \uses{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}
349	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], lemma-differential-seq (canonically
350	  % known as the right-exact conormal portion of tag 02JK; the
351	  % short-exact / split refinement is the proof step inside
352	  % lemma-separable-smooth) (read from
353	  % references/stacks-algebra.tex, L34087--34104 and L38724--38766).
354	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In diagram (\ref{equation-functorial-omega}),
355	  % suppose that $S \to S'$ is surjective with kernel $I \subset S$, and
356	  % assume that $R' = R$. Then there is a canonical exact sequence of
357	  % $S'$-modules
358	  % $$I/I^2 \longrightarrow \Omega_{S/R} \otimes_S S' \longrightarrow
359	  % \Omega_{S'/R} \longrightarrow 0$$
360	  % The leftmost map is characterized by the rule that $f \in I$ maps
361	  % to $\text{d}f \otimes 1$."
362	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Hence we get
363	  % $$\dim_{\kappa} \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa = \dim_\kappa
364	  % \mathfrak m/\mathfrak m^2 + \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) \geq \dim R +
365	  % \text{trdeg}_k (\kappa) = \dim_{\mathfrak q} S$$
366	  % (see Lemma \ref{lemma-dimension-at-a-point-finite-type-field} for
367	  % the last equality) with equality if and only if $R$ is regular."
368	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag~02JK (right-exact half = \texttt{lemma-differential-seq}; separable-residue refinement inside \texttt{lemma-separable-smooth}).}
369	  Let \(k\) be a field and let \(R\) be a Noetherian local
370	  \(k\)-algebra with maximal ideal \(\mathfrak m\) and residue field
371	  \(\kappa = R/\mathfrak m\). The canonical exact sequence
372	  \[
373	    \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
374	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa
375	    \;\longrightarrow\; \Omega_{\kappa / k}
376	    \;\longrightarrow\; 0
377	  \]
378	  identifies the cotangent space \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) (a
379	  \(\kappa\)-vector space) with the kernel of the right map. When
380	  \(\kappa / k\) is separable (in particular when \(k\) is
381	  algebraically closed and \(\kappa = k\), so \(\Omega_{\kappa / k}
382	  = 0\)) the leftmost map is injective and the sequence is short exact
383	  --- in fact split short exact when there is a \(k\)-algebra section
384	  \(\kappa \to R\) (e.g.\ at a closed point of a \(\bar k\)-variety).
385	  In particular, over an algebraically closed field \(k = \bar k\) and
386	  at a closed point \(\mathfrak m\),
387	  \[
388	    \Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa \;\simeq\; \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2
389	  \]
390	  as \(\kappa\)-vector spaces; hence
391	  \(\dim_\kappa(\Omega_{R/k} \otimes_R \kappa)
392	   = \dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\).
393	\end{lemma}
394	
395	\begin{lemma}
396	  [Stage 6.C --- Assembly: smooth standard-smooth stalk is regular local]
397	  \label{lem:stage6_regular_stalk_assembly}
398	  % NOTE (iter-199): the Lean target for this 6.C assembly is the body
399	  % of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isRegularLocalRing\_stalk\_of\_smooth}
400	  % itself (see \cref{lem:smooth_to_regular_local_ring}); the iter-198
401	  % blueprint named a planned \texttt{...\_aux} extraction that has not
402	  % been created in the Lean file. Treat the lemma below as the in-body
403	  % closure pattern that the existing helper consumes once Stages 6.A and
404	  % 6.B land, rather than as a separately-pinned Lean declaration.
405	  \uses{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim,
406	        lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula,
407	        lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field}
408	  Let \(\bar k\) be an algebraically closed field, let
409	  \(R \to S\) be a standard-smooth \(\bar k\)-algebra presentation of
410	  relative dimension \(n\) on an affine chart \(\Spec S \subseteq X\),
411	  and let \(z \in \Spec S\) be a point corresponding to a prime
412	  \(\mathfrak q \subset S\). Then the stalk \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) is a
413	  regular local ring of Krull dimension
414	  \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\). In the
415	  closed-point case (\(\kappa(\mathfrak q) = \bar k\),
416	  \(\mathrm{trdeg} = 0\)) the dimension equals \(n\); in the
417	  codim-\(1\) generic-point case (\(\mathrm{trdeg} = n - 1\)) the
418	  dimension equals \(1\).
419	\end{lemma}
420	
421	\begin{proof}
422	  Combine the three sub-lemmas:
423	  \begin{enumerate}
424	    \item By \cref{lem:rank_kaehler_localization_eq_relative_dim} (Stage~5b)
425	      the localised K\"ahler module \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q} / R}\)
426	      is free of rank \(n\) over \(S_{\mathfrak q}\).
427	    \item By \cref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula} (Stage 6.A,
428	      Stacks 00OE) the Krull dimension of \(S_{\mathfrak q}\) equals
429	      \(\dim_z \Spec S - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\),
430	      which for a standard-smooth presentation of relative dimension
431	      \(n\) equals \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\).
432	      (For the standard-smooth presentation the dimension
433	      \(\dim_z \Spec S = n\) follows from the global-complete-intersection
434	      structure: \(n\) variables modulo \(n - \dim\) equations, see
435	      Stacks \texttt{lemma-relative-global-complete-intersection-smooth}.)
436	    \item By \cref{lem:cotangent_kahler_over_field} (Stage 6.B,
437	      Stacks 02JK) the residue-field tensor \(\Omega_{S_{\mathfrak q}/R}
438	      \otimes_{S_{\mathfrak q}} \kappa(\mathfrak q)\) is isomorphic to
439	      \(\mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) as \(\kappa(\mathfrak q)\)-vector
440	      spaces. Combined with (1), \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak
441	      m / \mathfrak m^2 = n\) at the closed-point case (and in general
442	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\) by base-change
443	      of the standard-smooth presentation to the closed-point fibre).
444	    \item Compare (2) and (3): both \(\dim S_{\mathfrak q}\) and
445	      \(\dim_{\kappa(\mathfrak q)} \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2\) equal
446	      \(n - \mathrm{trdeg}_{\bar k}\,\kappa(\mathfrak q)\); the
447	      regularity criterion for Noetherian local rings (the equality
448	      \(\dim_\kappa \mathfrak m / \mathfrak m^2 = \dim R\) is the
449	      defining condition for a regular local ring, Stacks
450	      \texttt{definition-regular-local} \(=\) Mathlib
451	      \texttt{IsRegularLocalRing}) then gives \(S_{\mathfrak q}\)
452	      regular. The scheme-level transfer
453	      \(S_{\mathfrak q} = \mathcal O_{X, z}\) (Stage 3
454	      \texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime} on the affine chart) completes
455	      the conclusion.
456	  \end{enumerate}
457	\end{proof}

576	\subsection{Stage 6.A iter-200 substrate: Stacks 00OE 3-step decomposition}
577	\label{subsec:stage6_iib_substrate_iter200}
578	\textit{Source: \texttt{coe-stacks00oe} mathlib-analogist
579	cross-domain-inspiration recipe (iter-200, persistent
580	\texttt{analogies/coe-stacks00oe.md}); 7 axiom-clean
581	\texttt{private} substrate theorems landed in
582	\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} L688--L800 under
583	\texttt{namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme}.}
584	
585	The iter-200 Lane COE prover phase decomposed Stage 6.A
586	(\ref{lem:smooth_algebra_krull_dim_formula}, Stacks 00OE) into a
587	3-step Mathlib-chain. Steps 1 and 2 landed axiom-clean as
588	project-local \texttt{private} theorems; Step 3 landed in an
589	additive parameterised form awaiting the Jacobian-regular-sequence
590	witness for the relations of the smooth-algebra presentation.
591	
592	\paragraph{Step 1 --- Krull-dim \(\Rightarrow\) height (trivial,
593	\(\sim 1\) line).} Re-export of Mathlib's
594	\(\texttt{IsLocalization.AtPrime.ringKrullDim\_eq\_height}\) under
595	the project-local name
596	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_localization\_eq\_height\_atPrime}\); reduces
597	the localised Krull-dim goal to a height-of-maximal-ideal goal.
598	
599	\paragraph{Step 2 --- Polynomial-ring maximal-ideal height
600	(\(\sim 60\) LOC).} For \(\texttt{MvPolynomial}\,(\texttt{Fin}\,n)\,k\)
601	with \(k\) a field, every maximal ideal has height \emph{exactly}
602	\(n\). Lower bound:
603	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_ge\_card\_of\_field}\),
604	induction on \texttt{Fin n} via \(\texttt{MvPolynomial.finSuccEquiv}\)
605	+ Mathlib's \(\texttt{Polynomial.height\_eq\_height\_add\_one}\) plus
606	the Jacobson contraction
607	\(\texttt{Polynomial.isMaximal\_comap\_C\_of\_isJacobsonRing}\). Upper
608	bound:
609	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_le\_natCard\_of\_field}\)
610	via \(\texttt{Ideal.height\_le\_ringKrullDim\_of\_ne\_top}\) +
611	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.ringKrullDim\_of\_isNoetherianRing}\) +
612	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_eq\_zero\_of\_field}\). Combined: the
613	\texttt{Fin n}-form
614	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_eq\_card}\) and the
615	general \texttt{[Finite ι]}-form
616	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.maximalIdeal\_height\_eq\_natCard}\) via
617	\(\texttt{MvPolynomial.renameEquiv}\) +
618	\(\texttt{RingEquiv.height\_map}\). Capstone packaging the
619	Steps 1+2 composition for any localisation at a maximal ideal:

637	\paragraph{Iter-201+ Jacobian-regular-sequence witness recipe.} The
638	residual substrate of Stage 6.A is the Jacobian-criterion
639	regular-sequence witness for the relations of an
640	\(\texttt{Algebra.SubmersivePresentation}\).
641	
642	\textbf{API choice} (per iter-201 \texttt{progress-critic route201}
643	flag): the project-local target predicate is Mathlib's
644	\(\texttt{RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular}\), \emph{not}
645	\(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\). Reason: the iter-200 substrate
646	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_quotient\_add\_eq\_of\_regular\_sequence}\)
647	(\texttt{CodimOneExtension.lean} L807) consumes the strong predicate
648	\(\texttt{IsRegular}\) (via Mathlib's
649	\(\texttt{ringKrullDim\_add\_length\_eq\_ringKrullDim\_of\_isRegular}\));
650	the localised stalk \(R'_{m'}\) is local, so by Mathlib's
651	\(\texttt{IsLocalRing.isRegular\_iff\_isWeaklyRegular\_of\_subset\_maximalIdeal}\)
652	the two predicates are equivalent under the standing hypothesis that
653	the relations lie in the maximal ideal --- which holds because
654	the Jacobian-criterion conclusion is precisely that the relations
655	generate (a subset of) the maximal ideal at the closed point. The
656	recipe builds \(\texttt{IsWeaklyRegular}\) first (it is the
657	naturally-arising predicate from the Koszul-complex argument) and
658	then promotes to \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) via the bridge lemma; the
659	final consumer call uses the \(\texttt{IsRegular}\) form.
660

670	presentation form a Koszul-regular sequence at the closed point
671	because the Jacobian matrix is invertible modulo the maximal ideal

673	\mathfrak m_A^2\) are \(\kappa\)-linearly independent
674	\(\Rightarrow\) (Matsumura 14.2 / Stacks 00NQ on a regular local
675	ring) the \(f_j\) form a regular sequence in \(A\); (iii) bundle
676	as a 3-step chain:

99	**Reference**: iter-201 task report at
100	`task_results/archive/iter-201/AlgebraicJacobian_Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean.md`
101	lines 64--156 (Path B base case + inductive step decomposition);
102	`analogies/ab-stacks00mf.md` (persistent Path B verdict); Matsumura
103	§19 (Stacks 090V depth-pd formula), Bruns–Herzog Ch.1.
104	
105	**Recipe** (~80-120 LOC, per iter-201 prover scout):
106	
107	**Step 0 — Restructure**: rewrite `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`
108	to do `induction k generalizing M`. Generalize the surrounding setup
109	helpers (`exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing`,
110	`hasProjectiveDimensionLT_*`) so the IH is invocable on the kernel `K`
111	at smaller `k` inside the inductive step.
112	
113	**Step 1 — Base case `k = 0` (pd M = 1)** (~50-80 LOC):
114	1. Minimal surjection `f : R^n →ₗ M`,
115	   `HasProjectiveDimensionLT (ker f) 1` via iter-200 helpers.
116	2. `K := ker f` finite projective over local Noetherian R, hence free:
117	   `Module.Flat.of_projective` + `Module.free_of_flat_of_isLocalRing`.
118	   Pick basis `φ : (Fin m → R) ≃ₗ[R] K`.
119	3. `A := K.subtype ∘ₗ φ.toLinearMap : (Fin m → R) →ₗ R^n`, image `K`.
120	4. Every entry of `A` lies in `𝔪 = Ann_R κ`: use
121	   `ideal_smul_top_pi_const` (L863) + the minimality clause
122	   `ker f ⊆ 𝔪 • ⊤_{Fin n → R}` from
123	   `exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing`.
124	5. Apply `Module.ext_comp_mk₀_ofHom_eq_zero_of_entries_mem_annihilator`
125	   (iter-201 matrix-collapse, L1517) with `A`: every postcomposition
126	   `e.comp (mk₀ (ofHom A))` is zero in `Ext^p(κ, R^n)`.
127	6. Transport via `subtype = A ∘ₗ φ.symm.toLinearMap`: the
128	   postcomposition `Ext^p(κ, K) → Ext^p(κ, R^n)` (via SES first
129	   morphism) is also zero.
130	7. LES at `i = depth R - 1`: `Ext^{depth R - 1}(κ, M) →^δ
131	   Ext^{depth R}(κ, K)` is iso (next map zero by matrix-collapse +
132	   `Ext^{depth R - 1}(κ, R^n) = 0` since `depth R^n = depth R`).
133	   `Ext^{depth R}(κ, K) = Ext^{depth R}(κ, R^m) ≠ 0` (m ≥ 1,
134	   depth K = depth R). Conclude `depth M < depth R`, i.e.
135	   `depth M + 1 ≤ depth R`.
136	8. Matching `depth M + 1 ≥ depth R` from
137	   `depth_of_short_exact` part (2) on the SES.
138	9. Edge cases: `depth R = 0` (LES at i=0 contradiction); `m = 0`
139	   (i.e. `K = 0` → `f` iso → `M` projective → `pd M = 0`,
140	   contradicting `pd M = 1`).
141	
142	**Step 2 — Inductive step `k + 1, k ≥ 1`** (~30-50 LOC, no
143	matrix-collapse needed):
144	1. Minimal surjection `f`, `HasProjectiveDimensionLT K (k+1)`.
145	2. `pd K = k` exactly (contrapositive of
146	   `hasProjectiveDimensionLT_succ_of_hasProjectiveDimensionLT_ker`).
147	3. IH on `K` at smaller `k`: `k + depth K = depth R`, so
148	   `depth K = depth R - k`.
149	4. `depth_of_short_exact` part (2): `depth M ≥ depth K - 1 =
150	   depth R - k - 1`, so `depth M + k + 1 ≥ depth R`.
151	5. `depth_of_short_exact` part (3): `depth K ≥ min(depth R^n,
152	   depth M + 1) = min(depth R, depth M + 1)`. If `depth R ≤
153	   depth R - k`, impossible for k ≥ 1. So `depth M + 1 ≤ depth R -
154	   k`, i.e. `depth M + k + 1 ≤ depth R`.
155	6. Combine: `(k+1) + depth M = depth R`.
156	
157	**Step 3 — Promotions**: remove `private` from:
158	- `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` (this lemma; per blueprint
159	  iter-201 NOTE option (1)).
160	- `isDomain_of_regularLocal` (L2657; cross-file consumer = Lane COE
161	  Step A1 iter-203).
162	- `regularLocal_quotient_isRegularLocal_of_notMemSq` (L2293; same
163	  cross-file consumer).
164	
165	These promotions are purely cosmetic — the declarations are already

import Mathlib
open AlgebraicGeometry CategoryTheory TopologicalSpace
namespace Test

-- nonempty open in subsingleton-carrier scheme is ⊤
lemma open_eq_top_of_subsingleton {X : Scheme} [Subsingleton X]
    (U : X.Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) : U = ⊤ := by
  obtain ⟨⟨x, hx⟩⟩ := hU
  ext y
  simp only [Opens.coe_top, Set.mem_univ, iff_true]
  rwa [Subsingleton.elim y x]

-- full Γ(U) ≃+* kbar for nonempty U
noncomputable def gammaSpecField_ringEquiv (kbar : Type) [Field kbar]
    (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) :
    Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) ≃+* kbar := by
  have h : U = ⊤ := open_eq_top_of_subsingleton U hU
  subst h
  exact (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv

end Test
#print axioms Test.open_eq_top_of_subsingleton
#print axioms Test.gammaSpecField_ringEquiv

import Mathlib
set_option autoImplicit false
universe u
open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
namespace AlgebraicGeometry
namespace Scheme

private theorem exists_submersivePresentation_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    {n : ℕ} [h : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S] :
    ∃ (ix sx : Type), ∃ (_ : Finite sx) (_ : Finite ix),
      ∃ P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx, P.dimension = n :=
  h.out

private theorem isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen
    {X : Scheme.{u}} {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (z : X) (hzV : z ∈ V) :
    letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
    IsLocalization.AtPrime (X.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal :=
  hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩

private lemma open_eq_top_of_subsingleton {X : Scheme.{u}} [Subsingleton X]
    (U : X.Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) : U = ⊤ := by
  obtain ⟨⟨x, hx⟩⟩ := hU
  ext y
  simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_top, Set.mem_univ, iff_true]
  rwa [Subsingleton.elim y x]

noncomputable def gammaSpecField_ringEquiv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
    (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) :
    Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) ≃+* kbar := by
  have h : U = ⊤ := open_eq_top_of_subsingleton U hU
  subst h
  exact (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv

end Scheme
end AlgebraicGeometry
#print axioms AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.exists_submersivePresentation_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
#print axioms AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen
#print axioms AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.open_eq_top_of_subsingleton
#print axioms AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.gammaSpecField_ringEquiv

/-! ## §3.B. Project-local Mathlib supplement — Stage 6 scheme-to-algebra bridges (iter-202)

Lane COE Step B substrate: four axiom-clean scheme-to-algebra bridges feeding the
eventual closure of `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` (the L1061 Stacks-00TT
regularity gap). They are deliberately decoupled from Step A1 (the Matsumura
Jacobian-regular-sequence witness, gated on the iter-203 `AuslanderBuchsbaum`
public promotions) so they land independently this iter.

* **B.a** (`exists_submersivePresentation_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`):
  extract the explicit `Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` with `P.dimension = n`
  from `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S` (the relative-dimension
  packaging produced by the iter-198 sub-gap (i) discharger
  `exists_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension_of_isStandardSmooth`).
* **B.b** (`isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen`): the affine-open
  stalk-localisation `IsLocalization.AtPrime (stalk z) (primeIdealOf z)`, decoupled
  from standard-smoothness, as a named reusable substrate lemma.
* **B.c** (`open_eq_top_of_subsingleton` + `gammaSpecField_ringEquiv`): on a scheme
  whose carrier is a subsingleton (e.g.\ `Spec (.of k̄)` for a field `k̄`), every
  nonempty open is `⊤`; hence the sections of `Spec (.of k̄)` over any nonempty
  open form a ring isomorphic to `k̄`. This is the `Γ(Spec (.of k̄), U) = k̄`
  definitional bridge of the COE recipe, giving the algebraically-closed base ring
  of the standard-smooth presentation a clean `k̄`-identification.

`isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen` / `gammaSpecField_ringEquiv` are
exposed (non-`private`) since they are generic Mathlib-shaped facts a future
in-file or cross-file consumer may want; the rest are `private` helpers. -/

/-- **Lane COE Step B.a (iter-202).** Extract the explicit
`Algebra.SubmersivePresentation` underlying an
`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S` instance, together with the
witness `P.dimension = n`. This is a forward-ergonomics re-export of the
`Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.out` field, giving Step B.d direct
access to the submersive presentation `P` (whose relations are the input to the
iter-203 Step A1 Jacobian-regular-sequence witness). Axiom-clean: 1-line
re-export of the structure field. -/
private theorem exists_submersivePresentation_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension
    {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [Algebra R S]
    {n : ℕ} [h : Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension n R S] :
    ∃ (ix sx : Type), ∃ (_ : Finite sx) (_ : Finite ix),
      ∃ P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx, P.dimension = n :=
  h.out

/-- **Lane COE Step B.b (iter-202).** The affine-open stalk localisation: for an
affine open `V ∋ z` of a scheme `X`, the stalk `X.presheaf.stalk z` is the
localisation of the section ring `Γ(X, V)` at the prime ideal `primeIdealOf z`.
Re-export of `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk`, decoupled here from
standard-smoothness so downstream regularity computations (Step B.d) can name the
`IsLocalization.AtPrime` instance without re-deriving the Stage-3 algebra package.
Axiom-clean: 1-line re-export. -/
theorem isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen
    {X : Scheme.{u}} {V : X.Opens} (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (z : X) (hzV : z ∈ V) :
    letI := TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
    IsLocalization.AtPrime (X.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal :=
  hV.isLocalization_stalk ⟨z, hzV⟩

/-- **Lane COE Step B.c helper (iter-202).** On a scheme whose underlying carrier
is a subsingleton (the topological space has at most one point — e.g.\
`Spec (.of k̄)` for a field `k̄`, which has a unique point by
`PrimeSpectrum.instUnique`), every nonempty open subset equals `⊤`. Axiom-clean:
the unique-point argument via `Subsingleton.elim`. -/
private lemma open_eq_top_of_subsingleton {X : Scheme.{u}} [Subsingleton X]
    (U : X.Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) : U = ⊤ := by
  obtain ⟨⟨x, hx⟩⟩ := hU
  ext y
  simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_top, Set.mem_univ, iff_true]
  rwa [Subsingleton.elim y x]

/-- **Lane COE Step B.c (iter-202).** The `Γ(Spec (.of k̄), U) = k̄` bridge: for a
field `k̄` and any nonempty open `U` of `Spec (.of k̄)`, the section ring over `U`
is ring-isomorphic to `k̄`. Since `Spec (.of k̄)` has a subsingleton carrier
(`PrimeSpectrum.instUnique`), `U = ⊤` and the sections collapse to the global
sections `Γ(Spec (.of k̄), ⊤) ≅ k̄` (`Scheme.ΓSpecIso`). This supplies the
algebraically-closed base ring of the standard-smooth presentation
(`R = Γ(Spec (.of k̄), U)`) with a clean `k̄`-identification — the geometric input
to the closed-point residue-field bridge `finrank_cotangentSpace_of_bijective_algebraMap_residue`.
Axiom-clean: `subst` of `open_eq_top_of_subsingleton` + `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
noncomputable def gammaSpecField_ringEquiv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
    (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (hU : Nonempty U) :
    Γ(Spec (.of kbar), U) ≃+* kbar := by
  have h : U = ⊤ := open_eq_top_of_subsingleton U hU
  subst h
  exact (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv

/-- **Stacks `00TT`, Jacobian-criterion direction (regularity conclusion).**

  all axiom-clean (3 of the 4 sub-bridges; B.c landed as 2 declarations).

## B.a — `exists_submersivePresentation_of_isStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension`
- **Approach:** 1-line re-export of `Algebra.IsStandardSmoothOfRelativeDimension.out`.
  Statement returns `∃ (ix sx : Type), ∃ (_ : Finite sx) (_ : Finite ix),
  ∃ P : Algebra.SubmersivePresentation R S ix sx, P.dimension = n`.
- **API note:** the `.out` field fixes `ix sx : Type` (Type 0); stating with
  `Type*`/`Type u` gives a universe mismatch (`Exists.{2}` vs `Exists.{u+1}`).
  Use `Type` exactly.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.

## B.b — `isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen`
- **Approach:** re-export of `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_stalk` with the
  section-stalk algebra instance `TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk` set up via
  `letI`. Decouples the Stage-3 `IsLocalization.AtPrime` witness from
  standard-smoothness so it is callable as a standalone substrate fact.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — axiom-clean (verified by `lean_verify`).

## B.c — `open_eq_top_of_subsingleton` + `gammaSpecField_ringEquiv`
- **Approach:**
  - `open_eq_top_of_subsingleton`: a scheme with `[Subsingleton X]` carrier has
    every nonempty open `= ⊤` (`Subsingleton.elim` + `Opens.ext`).
  - `gammaSpecField_ringEquiv`: `Spec (.of kbar)` has subsingleton carrier
    (`PrimeSpectrum.instUnique` for a field, picked up automatically as
    `Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar))`); `subst` `U = ⊤`, then
    `Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar) : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) ≅ .of kbar` →
    `CategoryTheory.Iso.commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv` gives the `≃+* kbar`.
- **Result:** RESOLVED — both axiom-clean (`gammaSpecField_ringEquiv` verified by
  `lean_verify`; `open_eq_top_of_subsingleton` confirmed by `#print axioms` in an
  isolated `lean_run_code` run under the file's exact `open` context).
- **Why public:** `isLocalization_atPrime_stalk_of_affineOpen` and
  `gammaSpecField_ringEquiv` are generic Mathlib-shaped facts a future in-file /
  cross-file consumer may want; the rest are `private`.

- **Status:** NOT ADDED. Genuinely gated, not a search failure.

- **Next step (iter-203):** with the Lane AB promotions visible, build the Step A1

Any `-- TODO: try X`, `-- could use Y here`, or `/- Next step: ... -/` you write is a sign you stopped too early — attempt X/Y first; only leave the comment if the attempt fails with a named error. Stop only when progress is blocked by a specific, named obstacle in one of these categories:

When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature**. Three patterns are banned:
